text: Welcome to Fragmented, a software developer podcast where we talk about building good software and becoming better developers.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: My name's Don Felker.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And I'm Kaushik Gopal.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Welcome to the show.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Hey everybody, it's Don here.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It's been a while since I've been on the podcast and I want to say thank you for listening and continuing to listen.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Kaushik and I have recently chatted a little bit and decided that we're going to try to get back on a regular cadence to get the podcast out in a regular fashion so it's not so sporadic.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: We probably won't be on a weekly basis but we'll be a lot more frequent than we have been
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Probably in the last year, as most of you know and have experienced worldwide, the pandemic really shut a lot of stuff down and just changed the way of life for most people, including us drastically.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So that really impacted our ability to record.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And honestly, I killed some of the drive to record.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It's hard to record with so much wild and crazy things going on in the world.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Anyway, we're going to be back.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So thanks again for listening.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But the reason why I'm here today is because I wanted to talk to you about the concept of quality over quantity.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And it is a very abstract term, and it's one that we've all heard many times.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But it's something that during yesterday, I had a conversation with a friend, and she said that quality over quantity.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And she was talking about just friends in general and the relationships everybody has between themselves.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And she was basically saying, look,
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I'm going to only favor quality over quantity.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Meaning that if you're going to invite people into your life, it could be friends, family, whatever, the quality time that you have is way more valuable than the quantity of time.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So let's say you hang out with your best friend
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: one day and you go out and you go on some adventure together and it's a adventure that you remember for the rest of your life.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Maybe you were only doing this thing for 12 hours, but you kayaked down a whitewater river.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: That's something you're going to remember for the rest of your life.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: That's a quality event.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Now, if you hang out with that same friend every single day and you're
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: you know, 95% of the time you're just around each other, not doing anything of any consequence.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: That's just quantity.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I'm not saying it's not valuable, but with how short life is, the quantity, the quality of the time that you have and what you do with it is what really matters.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And that, and this relates to software.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: really deeply because it really got me thinking about what else does this apply to?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And it started getting me think about developing software.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And a couple of weeks ago, I was contracted by a company, excuse me, contacted by a company to consult for them for basically 40 hours.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Actually, it was just 20 hours at first.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: They wanted me to do something in 20 hours.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: We had a phone call on a Friday afternoon or Friday morning and basically needed to have something done by next week.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So I literally had two or three days to implement something.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And this immediately made me think of, okay, what can I do to really push the needle forward?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And to make a long story short, they needed an application that ran on iOS and Android, and they wanted to use this application as a sales tool for their sales team so that they could sell some clients and obtain some larger contracts.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: and build out a new product.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So that was the reasoning.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: At that point in time, I decided, well, the best tool for me to build this in is it needed to be a mobile app.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So I wrote it in Flutter and simply because it allowed me to build both iOS and Android at the same time.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I didn't need deep system integration.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It was forms over data and I needed to get it done quickly.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So I embarked on that.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And where the quality versus quantity comes in here is I spent the next 24 hours, so two 12-hour days, building this Flutter app on the weekend to get it done for this client.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And those 24 hours were some of the most productive 24 hours I've had.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: to get into the state, and I was able to build an application that they wanted, that far surpassed their expectations, that gave them everything they needed and more within that 24 hour period using a tool like Flutter.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Now I'm not here to discuss the benefits or the negative aspects of Flutter or anything like that.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: That's not this discussion.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: This discussion is more along the lines of quality.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: What was I able to do with that time?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: How was able to maximize that time?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And I was able to do that just using that tool.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But really what it came down to was focus and the quality of time I've had.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: You know, think back how many times have you built an application or worked on a side project and been sitting there cranking away at work on it and you'll spend eight hours, 10 hours, a week, two weeks, three weeks on this project.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: You're perfecting pixel perfect border radiuses and making sure this animation flows left to right perfectly.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And before you know it, you spent weeks and weeks and weeks on it and you still haven't shipped it.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: You're putting in quantity at that point.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I'm sure you're doing some quality work, but
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Let's take the other side of the coin.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: How many times have you sat down and entered what's known as the flow state?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And the flow state is where you literally feel like you're part of the machine.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: You sit down to code and you have something you need to do.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Maybe, you know, I've perfected a way and I'll share here at the end how I get into flow state.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: for me.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But let's say you get into the code and you start working on the project and for whatever reason, the hours melt by and all of a sudden it's time to go home or it's time to tend to other responsibilities in life and you have to wrap it up for the day.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And you look back and you think,
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: whoa, what just happened?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I just wrote an insane amount of code or I figured out this very difficult problem.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And now this is done.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And this has been something I've been trying to do or figure out or have been tossing around my head for months now.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And I just sat down and did it.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And then sometimes you think like, man, I should have done this a long time ago.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Like, what was I waiting for?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Like, I had it in me the whole time.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And that's the quality work is when you're in that flow state, that's the quality.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And that's what this initial conversation with my friend yesterday got me thinking about is that quality versus quantity is so crucial.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: You have to figure out a way to create that and manifest that time so you can get into that quality, that flow state of development.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Because...
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: A lot of people work, I mean the majority of the world works at a job and at these jobs you'll sit there and just waste away the time.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: You'll work on a feature or project and then two or three hours later will go by and you've maybe checked some email or you've looked at social media or read an article and you've basically just been distracted and you're waiting for the time to pass.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: That's the quantity.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: That's the quantity of time.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And I think a lot of people, unfortunately, confuse the quantity of time that they work on something with the quality of time they work on something.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Now, I'm not saying this 24-hour project is something that's this fantastic thing that I've written.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: No, there's a lot of edge cases that need to be handled still.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But for what I was able to get done in it, it really brought it home like, wow.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: You can get a lot accomplished if you just focus on quality of just the quality of time.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: How much effort am I putting into this?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Am I eliminating my distractions?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Am I entering a flow state?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Am I just really plowing ahead?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Am I not getting stuck in the minutia of a project?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It's amazing what you can get done.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And so some of the things that I did to do that was when I was writing this Flutter application, I needed to find a screen or a design that looked good for something.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I would quickly go look at a few examples and I time boxed everything.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I'm not doing this for more than 10 minutes.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: that might be finding a good looking design for a detail screen.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And I would just kind of look at a few examples online.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Okay, that looks good.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Or I'd see a YouTube tutorial of someone doing it.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I'd watch it real quick and I'd mimic it and then tweak it to my satisfactory, you know, my satisfaction.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And then I'd ship it.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And that screen was done.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Okay.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Maybe it wasn't perfect.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Maybe the padding wasn't just perfect.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I'll come back and fix that later.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But at least now I've got this screen done.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It works.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It's functional.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It provides value.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It's not pixel perfect.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But if you ask any,
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Typical user on the street, do you see anything wrong with this screen?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: 99% of them would say no.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It's just the fact that we have this innate ability to see, oh, that, you know, that back icon looks a little too big or this shade of blue doesn't really match.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And we, or this button is just too big or it's in the wrong location.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And I had a perfect example of this.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I needed to create a basically an account screen and I needed to create a logout button.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: and I had the screen laid out and I added the logout button, but for some reason, due to the way I had laid it out and the way that Flutter was rendering it, I couldn't get the button to look how I wanted to.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I spent about five minutes on it and I had to check myself.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I said, all right, is this, am I spending quality time here?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Am I just wasting a quantity on time of what this logout button looks like?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Like right now, this doesn't really matter to the huge feature set of the project for the goal that I'm trying to accomplish.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So,
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: That's what I did is I just made the button look okay.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It works.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It's in the general location I want it to be.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It's blue.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Cool.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It has ripple and click state.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Awesome.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It works.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It allows me to log out onto the next screen.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And my main goal was to, I had a list of screens I had to get done.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And I told myself, look, I'm going to bang through each one of these screens.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I'm going to get them operational.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: They're going to look decent.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And then I'm going to come back and refine them.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Because if I stay on one screen for far too long and I spend a ton of quantity time on it, just perfecting it to make that, you know, what feels like a good quality, perfect pixel, perfect screen, I didn't get everything else done I needed to get done.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And I have no idea, you know, for this project that I was on, I have no idea if that screen's gonna matter that much.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I need to meet the expectations of the client, which for me was just, you know, building something that looks good and works.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: They don't care that it's Flutter.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: They don't care if it's React Native.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: They don't care if it's native Android or native iOS.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: They want to make sure that it works for their use case.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And so what I'm trying to say is here, you just kind of have to, you know, the whole Gordian knot analogy.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So for those of you that are not familiar with the, you know, the Gordian knot, I recommend just looking up the Gordian knot.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And basically what it is, is the, at a real high level, it's, if you have a very complex bunch of things, something complex you need to do.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: you just need to find the simplest way to do it and in this example there was a very complex knot that people could not untie and basically it was hey if whoever can untie the knot will get this fantastic thing and some some mythology thing we'll get this fantastic thing and no one could undo it and then finally somebody came by and said look well i can do it and they said how and the person pulled out their sword and they just cut the knot and they said okay great you get this fantastic thing and no one ever really thought like oh
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: we were trying to untie this knot like why we never thought to just slice right through it so that's what i'm trying to get at here is that you kind of have to remove a lot of the complexity of what you're working on inside of an application sometimes and just focus on getting something done being very productive with your time if you have that quality time
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Now, again, I'm not saying you have to produce 10 screens in five days or two days like, you know, like I was doing with this prototype app and this proof of concept app.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But what I am saying is that maybe you have this feature that you're working on and you need to get it done.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: What I will typically do at this point is I will first get a screen fully operational, meaning that the screen will do all of the function that has.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And it may look like absolute dog crap.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It looks terrible.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I mean, it's horrendous.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It looks like a programmer UI.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It's just non-styled text boxes, labels, not even half times, not even labels, buttons.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But if you knew how to use it, it would provide value for what you need to do.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Then at that time, I go back through, okay, well now let me add some color or add some spacing.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Okay, now let me apply the styles to the buttons and input fields.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Okay, let me add the input labels and all of the...
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: you know, internationalization and all of whatever the features you have is.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But that's how I build these different screens.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And what that does allows me to focus on the core principle of what I'm trying to do first, which is solve a problem.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And then I'm going to make it look nice because the problem is if you go the other way around and you make something look nice right away.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: a lot of people are gonna think that you're done.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And if you're already done, if they think you're done, then there's an expectation, especially if they're non-technical, that why are you spending all this time on this thing?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: It's not done yet.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And so that's kind of something that I do, especially with a lot of consulting clients is I'll build it, I'll have it working, I'll show them progress.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Hey, this is working.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Now we're gonna spruce it up a bit.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Now we're gonna make it look good.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And that's the practice that I follow a lot of the times.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But to kind of bring this full circle,
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: What you're really looking for here is that quality over quantity time.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So if you find yourself just letting the hours waste away and you're like, well, I've been working on, you know, I worked on this project for
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: 85 hours already or you know a whole week or seven weeks or 10 weeks ask yourself did i really work on it that long or did i just sit in front of the computer and watch the time go by because that's what i was supposed to do during this time i was supposed to just sit here until five o'clock because i get in at eight or nine and i'm just waiting for the time to pass so i can go home
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Are you doing that?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Or are you actually focused on work?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And maybe you're caught up on work.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: What do you do then?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Well, you can always go learn something new.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: You can always value your time because time is the one resource that is unrenewable.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: You'll never get back today.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And to kind of leave you with a little anecdote, I guess what it would be called, is this one kind of rocked me recently is
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Today is the youngest you'll ever be.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Think about that.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Today is the youngest you'll ever be.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So we always think about, well, I'll do that tomorrow.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Well, you know, I got youth on my side or I have some more time.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: But...
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Today is the youngest you're going to be.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So use your time wisely.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Focus on the quality time of your work, of your interpersonal relationships, of everything.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Does it mean that going to the gym and working out for three hours is the best thing?
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: No.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Sometimes you can hit the stimulus of what you're trying to do.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And half hour to 45 minutes, because that's what you needed to do that day.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Other times you might be doing long form, low intensity, low intensity cardiovascular training at a very low heart rate.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And that might require you to be active on multiple different cardio machines for 45 minutes to an hour and a half for whatever you're training for.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So the thing is, is quantity of things does not mean quality.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Quantity does not mean quality.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: You can get high quality in a very short period of time.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So just something to think about.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Thanks for listening, folks.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: Kaushik and I are gonna try to be back a lot more.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And things should be coming back around.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: So again, we'll be talking about various different software topics, not just mobile development.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: A lot of the times, for example, after this call, this call, this recording, I'm going to be hopping into some Rails app.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: I'm building a different SaaS app today and I'm going to try to get shipped.
speaker: SPEAKER_00

text: And so we'll be sharing a lot of those things about so forth.
speaker: SPEAKER_00
